title: As playoffs near, doubt the 2025-26 Bruins at your own risk
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Boston Bruins overcame a three-goal deficit to defeat the Columbus Blue Jackets 4-3 in a shootout.
Jeremy Swayman made crucial stops, maintaining a nearly 92% playoff qualification prediction for the Bruins.
Pavel Zacha and Viktor Arvidsson highlighted the comeback with key scoring plays, including the equalizer and shootout goals.

title: Dating back to the 1600s, the second-oldest home in Cambridge lists for $5.8M
author: Megan Johnson
bullets: The Hooper-Lee-Nichols House, built circa 1685, is listed for $5.8 million in Cambridge.
The property, headquarters of History Cambridge for 70 years, is protected by a preservation restriction.
The historic home features Georgian, Victorian, and Colonial Revival alterations and spans 6,449 square feet.

title: Jayson Tatum looked like a star again as Celtics roll past Hornets: 7 takeaways
author: Tom Westerholm
bullets: Jayson Tatum scored 32 points with eight assists in the Celtics' 114-99 win over the Hornets.
Payton Pritchard added 28 points and six assists on 10-for-18 shooting in a starting role.
Ron Harper Jr. scored seven points before twisting his ankle in the second quarter against Charlotte.

title: Jaylen Brown boasts on social media after Celtics clinch playoff berth for 12th straight season
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Celtics achieved their 12th consecutive playoff berth with a 114-99 win over the Hornets in Charlotte.
Jaylen Brown celebrated the Celtics' 50-win season on social media, despite missing the game with Achilles tendinitis.
Jayson Tatum scored a season-high 32 points, contributing to Boston's victory as they continue to contend despite injuries.

title: Connelly Early continues to shine for Red Sox after strong first start in 2026
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Connelly Early has a 2.19 career ERA in five MLB starts with the Boston Red Sox.
In his first 2026 start, Early pitched 5.1 innings, allowing one run on five hits with six strikeouts.
Early was initially outside the rotation but filled in due to Ranger Suarez's delayed ramp-up after WBC.

title: Boston Fleet cinch berth in Walter Cup Playoffs with win over Minnesota Frost
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Boston Fleet secured a spot in the 2026 Walter Cup Playoffs after defeating Minnesota Frost 4-2.
Boston Fleet is the first team to clinch a playoff berth, achieving this faster than any other team in league history.
Aerin Frankel led the Fleet's success with a 1.17 GAA, while Amanda Thiele debuted in net against Minnesota.

title: Woman charged in fatal South Boston stabbing
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Gisselle Pascual, 24, was charged with manslaughter in connection to a fatal stabbing in South Boston.
The victim was found at 258 Old Colony Ave. and died the next day at a local hospital.
Pascual's charges were upgraded from assault to manslaughter after the victim's death, police said.

title: Brockton police shoot man who allegedly fired at officers, man sleeping nearby also injured, DA says
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Brockton police officers shot a 44-year-old man in the shoulder after alleged gunfire during a vehicle pursuit.
A 71-year-old man sleeping nearby was grazed by a bullet and sustained non-life-threatening injuries.
Massachusetts State Police are investigating the incident, and a handgun was recovered at the scene.

title: Former Bruins coach Bruce Cassidy abruptly fired by Vegas before playoffs
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Bruce Cassidy was fired by the Vegas Golden Knights less than a month before the Stanley Cup Playoffs.
Vegas GM Kelly McCrimmon named John Tortorella as the new head coach of the Golden Knights.
Vegas is third in the Pacific Division with a 36-26-16 record, struggling with recent losses and goalie issues.

title: New NFL mock draft has Patriots addressing major need on defense with 31st pick
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Patriots may pick Clemson's T.J. Parker, an edge rusher, as the 31st overall pick in the 2026 NFL Draft.
T.J. Parker recorded 21.5 sacks and 126 tackles in three seasons at Clemson, potentially filling a defensive gap for the Patriots.
New England aims to enhance their pass rush after recording only 35 team sacks last season, adding Dre'Mont Jones last month.
